source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The maximum frequency of the signal \( m(t) \) is less than \( f_{\mathrm{m}} \). If \( m(t) \) is sampled using \( q(t) \) as shown in <image>, determine the expression for the spectrum of the sampled signal.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: \( M_{\mathrm{s}}(\omega) = \frac{\tau}{T} \sum_{n=-\infty}^{\infty} \text{Sa}^2\left(2 \pi n \tau f_{\mathrm{m}}\right) M\left(\omega - 4\pi n f_{\mathrm{m}}\right) \)

choice_map.A: \( M_{\mathrm{s}}(\omega) = \frac{\tau}{T} \sum_{n=-\infty}^{\infty} \text{Sa}^2\left(\pi n \tau f_{\mathrm{m}}\right) M\left(\omega - 2\pi n f_{\mathrm{m}}\right) \)
choice_map.B: \( M_{\mathrm{s}}(\omega) = \frac{\tau}{T} \sum_{n=-\infty}^{\infty} \text{Sa}^2\left(4 \pi n \tau f_{\mathrm{m}}\right) M\left(\omega - \pi n f_{\mathrm{m}}\right) \)
choice_map.C: \( M_{\mathrm{s}}(\omega) = \frac{\tau}{T} \sum_{n=-\infty}^{\infty} \text{Sa}^2\left(3\pi n \tau f_{\mathrm{m}}\right) M\left(\omega - 2\pi n f_{\mathrm{m}}\right) \)
choice_map.D: \( M_{\mathrm{s}}(\omega) = \frac{\tau}{T} \sum_{n=-\infty}^{\infty} \text{Sa}^2\left(2 \pi n \tau f_{\mathrm{m}}\right) M\left(\omega - 4\pi n f_{\mathrm{m}}\right) \)
